paper.title: ImageWAM: Do World Action Models Really Need Video Generation, or Just Image Editing?

research_directions.direction: Multi-step closed-loop editing rollouts with temporal consistency
research_directions.seed_span: Extending ImageWAM to iteratively predict the next endpoint frame as the observation changes, enabling closed-loop re-planning
research_directions.motivation: Current ImageWAM predicts a single endpoint frame t+H+1 from current observation. For long-horizon tasks, or when the action chunk fails, re-planning requires re-running the entire pipeline. An iterative editing approach would maintain temporal coherence while keeping the efficiency advantage.
key_questions: How to maintain editing consistency across multiple rollouts?
When to trigger re-planning vs. continue execution?
How does error accumulate over multiple editing steps?
research_directions.expected_impact: Enables truly closed-loop world-action reasoning with temporal awareness
research_directions.possible_start: Add lightweight temporal module (e.g., temporal attention or state-space model) on top of editing caches from consecutive timesteps

research_directions.direction: Quantitative analysis of editing cache information content
research_directions.seed_span: Developing quantitative metrics for what information editing caches encode
research_directions.motivation: Current attention analysis is qualitative (Figure 4). Understanding precisely what information is in the editing caches — and how it relates to action prediction — would enable principled improvements to the architecture.
key_questions: What is the mutual information between editing cache and optimal action?
Which layers contribute most to action-relevant features?
Can we intervene on caches to probe causal relationships?
research_directions.expected_impact: Principled understanding of why editing works, enabling better cache design
research_directions.possible_start: Apply probing classifiers, activation patching, or representational similarity analysis on editing caches at different denoising timesteps

research_directions.direction: Video editing as a middle ground between image editing and full video generation
research_directions.seed_span: Exploring video editing (edit a video clip rather than a single image) for WAMs
research_directions.motivation: Image editing captures a single transformation; full video generation captures all dynamics. Video editing — editing a short clip — could capture task-relevant temporal changes (e.g., object moving to a target) without full scene modeling overhead.
key_questions: Can video editing models provide better temporal information than single-frame editing?
What is the efficiency-cost tradeoff (video editing vs image editing vs video generation)?
Which tasks particularly benefit from temporal change modeling?
research_directions.expected_impact: Bridges the gap between efficiency (image editing) and temporal awareness (video generation)
research_directions.possible_start: Adapt existing video editing models (e.g., vid2vid, InstructVideo) using the same cache-extraction approach

research_directions.seed_span: Systematic study of how editing backbone scaling affects policy performance
research_directions.motivation: Initial evidence (Table 7) shows FLUX.2 9B (+2.1 pts over 4B). Understanding the scaling behavior — does editing pretraining benefit more from scale than standard vision backbones? — would guide investment in model development.
key_questions: Does editing backbone scale better than video generation backbone for policy learning?
What is the marginal return of adding parameters to the editing model vs. the action expert?
At what scale does editing pretraining saturate for robot policy tasks?
research_directions.expected_impact: Resource allocation guidance for WAM development
research_directions.possible_start: Train editing models at multiple scales (e.g., 0.5B, 1B, 3B, 7B) and measure policy performance

research_directions.direction: Combining editing-based reasoning with 3D scene representations
research_directions.seed_span: Integrating image editing with 3D representations for tasks requiring explicit geometric reasoning
research_directions.motivation: Current ImageWAM operates in 2D image space. Many manipulation tasks require 3D reasoning (spatial relationships, grasping orientation, collision avoidance). Combining editing features with 3D representations (point clouds, NeRF, 3D Gaussians) could enable geometrically grounded action prediction.
key_questions: Can editing caches provide useful 3D-aware features?
How to fuse 2D editing features with 3D representations?
Does 3D grounding particularly help geometrically challenging tasks?
research_directions.expected_impact: Enables geometrically precise manipulation from editing-based reasoning
research_directions.possible_start: Add 3D positional encodings to the action expert's cross-attention with editing caches

research_directions.direction: Goal-conditioned exploration using editing models as intrinsic reward
research_directions.seed_span: Using the editing model's ability to predict task-relevant visual changes as a reward signal for RL exploration
research_directions.motivation: Image editing models encode 'what should change under instruction.' This change signal could serve as a dense reward for reinforcement learning — rewarding transitions that move the scene toward an instruction-consistent target state.
key_questions: Can editing-based change detection serve as a task-agnostic exploration bonus?
How to handle false positives (spurious changes not related to the task)?
Does editing-based reward scale to complex, long-horizon tasks?
research_directions.expected_impact: Enables RL agents to learn from editing-defined task progress without hand-crafted rewards
research_directions.possible_start: Use editing cache similarity (current vs. target) as reward signal in a simple manipulation environment

research_directions.seed_span: Applying ImageWAM to different robot platforms and embodiments beyond dual-arm
research_directions.motivation: Current validation is on dual-arm platform (Dobot XTrainer). Testing on single-arm, mobile manipulators, and humanoids would assess generality. The editing-based representation should be embodiment-agnostic since it models scene change rather than robot-specific dynamics.
key_questions: Does the editing representation transfer across embodiments without fine-tuning?
What domain gap exists between simulator-trained and real-world-embodied editing models?
Can a single editing WAM serve multiple embodiments?
research_directions.expected_impact: Demonstrates generality of editing-based world-action modeling across platforms
research_directions.possible_start: Evaluate pretrained ImageWAM on standard single-arm benchmarks (e.g., MetaWorld, Franka Kitchen)

research_directions.direction: Causal intervention analysis: isolating editing cache contributions
research_directions.seed_span: Developing intervention-based methods to understand which cache components drive action predictions
research_directions.motivation: Current understanding of 'editing caches encode transformation information' is based on attention visualization. Causal intervention (e.g., ablating specific cache components, perturbing cache values, swapping caches across different task conditions) would provide stronger evidence and reveal failure modes.
key_questions: Which layers' KV caches are most critical for action prediction?
Can we compress editing caches with minimal performance loss?
What happens when editing cache encodes wrong transformation?
research_directions.expected_impact: Principled guidance for cache design and compression
research_directions.possible_start: Apply attention knockout, representational perturbation, or cache resampling experiments

research_directions.direction: Instruction ambiguity handling through multi-modal editing conditioning
research_directions.seed_span: Robust handling of ambiguous or underspecified natural language instructions in editing-based policy
research_directions.motivation: Image editing models assume unambiguous instructions. Real-world robot instructions can be underspecified ('clean up') or ambiguous ('move it there'). The editing backbone must handle this ambiguity and the action expert must still produce reasonable behavior.
key_questions: How does instruction ambiguity affect editing cache quality?
Can we detect when the editing model is producing uncertain transformations?
Should the policy act conservatively under instruction ambiguity?
research_directions.expected_impact: More robust real-world deployment with diverse natural language instructions
research_directions.possible_start: Add uncertainty quantification to editing cache; test with systematically varied instruction specificity

research_directions.direction: Training-free adaptation of editing models for new robot tasks
research_directions.seed_span: Adapting ImageWAM to new tasks without fine-tuning, using in-context editing capabilities
research_directions.motivation: Current ImageWAM requires task-specific fine-tuning. Advanced image editing models can perform zero-shot or in-context editing from examples. This capability could be leveraged for fast task adaptation without gradient updates.
key_questions: Can in-context editing (providing example edits) serve as task specification?
How far can we push zero-shot action prediction from editing priors?
What is the minimal demonstration set needed for new tasks?
research_directions.expected_impact: Enables rapid task acquisition without per-task training
research_directions.possible_start: Evaluate ImageWAM with frozen editing backbone on novel tasks using in-context examples

meta_analysis.dominant_theme: Understanding and extending editing-based representations for world-action modeling
meta_analysis.methodological_gap: Quantitative understanding of what editing caches encode and how to best leverage them
meta_analysis.theoretical_gap: Formal characterization of when image editing beats video generation for WAMs — what task properties favor each approach?
